mnemonics: RIP Thread: Runnable (Interface), Inherit (extends Thread), Pool (ExecutorService)
The JVM is a Stage: Threads are the actors performing on a single CPU stage (core), managed by a scheduler (director).
Sleep vs. Wait: Sleep (Thread) naps on the job, Wait (Object) waits for a notify cue.
keyPoints: Threads can be created by extending the Thread class or implementing the Runnable interface.
The start() method is used to begin execution of a thread, which internally calls the run() method.
Synchronization is used to control access to shared resources and prevent thread interference and memory consistency errors.
commonMistakes: Calling run() directly instead of start(), which executes the code in the current thread instead of a new one.
Not properly synchronizing access to shared mutable data, leading to race conditions.
Forgetting that wait(), notify(), and notifyAll() are called on the object used as the lock, not the Thread itself.
